body:
## Summary
- Add `.agent/sepo-version.json` for installed Sepo version/source identity.
- Add typed metadata validation/loading helpers and a `print-sepo-version` CLI for workflows and diagnostics.
- Document the SemVer policy, metadata schema, and fork/install source-identity model in setup/install docs.

## Tests
- `npm --prefix .agent run build`
- `cd .agent && node --test dist/__tests__/sepo-version.test.js`
- `node .agent/dist/cli/print-sepo-version.js --json`
- `git diff --check`
- `env -u BASE_BRANCH -u BASE_PR npm --prefix .agent test`

Closes #127

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

implementation succeeded; dispatching review

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777863008787 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyOTgzOTg1Mjc6aW1wbGVtZW50OjEyNzpyZXZpZXc6MTI4OjM -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Adds explicit metadata fields in `.agent/sepo-version.json` and validates each field/type separately in `.agent/src/sepo-version.ts`; CLI uses separate `--path` and `--json` flags rather than overloaded input strings. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | New CLI reuses the existing shared `setOutput` helper from `.agent/src/output.ts` instead of reimplementing GitHub Actions output writing. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates install/setup docs and adds `.agent/docs/technical-details/versioning.md` covering SemVer policy, metadata schema, source identity, and CLI usage. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR scope matches linked issue #127 and parent #74: foundation only, with updater rewriting, bug-report integration, and release workflows deferred. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | New docs describe policy/schema concepts and include a schema field table, but do not add a stale per-file inventory table. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is limited to version metadata, validation/CLI/test files, and directly related docs. No unrelated workflow or runtime refactors. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Implementation is a small typed module plus focused CLI/tests; it avoids new dependencies or premature release/update automation. |

## Notes

- Strongest score drivers: the PR stays aligned with the child-task boundary from #127, keeps runtime logic in typed `.agent/src` code, and updates docs in the same change.
- I did not score unrelated orchestration, authorization, preview, idempotent automation, or merge-flow rubrics because this PR does not change those behaviors.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #128 is the first scoped child PR for issue #74 via #127.
- It adds the installed Sepo version metadata foundation: `.agent/sepo-version.json`, typed validation/loading helpers, and a `print-sepo-version` CLI for workflows and diagnostics.
- It documents the SemVer policy, metadata schema, and fork/install source-identity model.
- The PR intentionally leaves update workflow rewriting, bug-report integration, and release prepare/publish automation for later child work.

## Review
The PR is well-scoped and aligned with the parent plan. The remaining concern is a small validation hardening issue in the new metadata contract; no blocking defects were identified.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Validate `source_ref` syntax | WARNING | `.agent/src/sepo-version.ts` accepts any non-empty `source_ref`, including internal whitespace or control characters. This should be tightened before later workflows and bug reports depend on the value. |
| Optional polish and future-proofing notes | INFO | Reviewers also noted optional cleanup around fallback comments, SHA casing, whitespace-only error text, CLI output breadth, and future RC generalization. None appears required for this foundation PR. |

## Progress
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific findings.
- Reviewer-reported verification passed: build, focused version tests, full `.agent` test suite, and `git diff --check`.
- One inline comment was posted for the actionable `source_ref` validation issue.
- A proposed `channel` / `installed_from` cross-field restriction is not carried forward because #74 explicitly supports v0.x pre-release release-tag installs.
- The `1.0.0-rc.N` validator shape matches the current issue scope; broader future major-version RC support can be deferred.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Validate `source_ref` syntax</summary>

**Cause:**  
[`source_ref`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/86cb1e4afade85885044b1f8c40dd704d1141441/.agent/src/sepo-version.ts#L173) is validated only through `requireStringField`, so values with internal whitespace/control characters can still pass.

**Candidate solutions:**  
Add a `SOURCE_REF_RE` or equivalent validation helper that rejects whitespace, control characters, and clearly invalid git-ref syntax. Add focused tests for invalid refs such as embedded spaces, newlines, and unsafe ref punctuation.

**Comments:**  
This is low-risk to fix now and avoids propagating malformed installed identity data into future workflow outputs, summaries, and bug-report metadata.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: one unresolved validation hardening issue requires a concrete branch change and is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Add `source_ref` validation and focused tests rejecting whitespace/control characters or invalid git-ref syntax.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

review verdict is minor_issues; dispatching fix-pr

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777863476582 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyOTg2MTIwMDk6cmV2aWV3OjEyODpmaXhfcHI6MTI4OjQ -->

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-127/codex-25298398527`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

- Addressed the latest review synthesis action item by adding git-ref-like `source_ref` validation in `.agent/src/sepo-version.ts`.
- Added focused tests for valid branch/tag/ref values and invalid whitespace, control-character, and punctuation cases.
- Updated versioning docs so the `source_ref` schema describes the stricter contract.
- Checks: `npm --prefix .agent run build`; `node --test .agent/dist/__tests__/sepo-version.test.js`; `git diff --check`.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 5/10).

PR fixes succeeded; dispatching review

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 7 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Prefer explicit structured inputs | pass | 7/7 | Adds discrete metadata fields in [`.agent/sepo-version.json`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/6eb244410d18d8f5b8c08cd969ec4c7ad27849cb/.agent/sepo-version.json) and validates each field in [`.agent/src/sepo-version.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/6eb244410d18d8f5b8c08cd969ec4c7ad27849cb/.agent/src/sepo-version.ts); CLI uses explicit `--path` and `--json` flags. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | New CLI reuses the shared `setOutput` helper from [`.agent/src/output.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/main/.agent/src/output.ts) instead of writing Actions outputs ad hoc. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates setup/install docs and adds [versioning docs](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/6eb244410d18d8f5b8c08cd969ec4c7ad27849cb/.agent/docs/technical-details/versioning.md) covering SemVer policy, schema, source identity, and CLI behavior. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR scope matches linked issue #127 and parent #74: version metadata foundation only, with update rewriting, bug-report integration, and release workflows deferred. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | New docs describe the versioning policy and schema fields; they do not add a stale per-file inventory table. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is limited to version metadata, typed helper/CLI/tests, and directly related docs. No unrelated workflow/runtime refactors. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Implementation is a small typed module, a focused CLI, and targeted tests; no new dependency or premature release/update automation was added. |

## Notes

- The current PR head includes the follow-up `source_ref` validation fix, plus focused tests and docs for the stricter contract.
- I did not score unrelated orchestration, authorization, preview, idempotency, status-comment, or merge-flow rubrics because this PR does not change those behaviors.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #128 is the version metadata foundation child PR for #74, tracked through #127.
- It adds committed Sepo install metadata, typed validation/loading helpers, and a workflow-facing `print-sepo-version` CLI.
- It updates setup/install/versioning docs so future update, release, and bug-report work can rely on a shared installed identity contract.
- The PR correctly keeps update rewriting, bug-report payload integration, and release automation out of this first slice.

## Review
The current head has addressed the earlier `source_ref` validation finding and remains well-scoped. One small contract clarity issue remains around path resolution behavior; it is not blocking, but it should be cleaned up before merge.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Document and test metadata path fallback | WARNING | `resolveSepoVersionMetadataPath` silently falls back to `sepo-version.json` in the current working directory when `.agent/sepo-version.json` is missing. If that fallback is intentional for runs from `.agent/`, it should be documented and covered by a focused test. |
| Optional consistency and docs polish | INFO | One reviewer flagged uppercase `source_sha` acceptance, `schema_version` output docs, `rc.N >= 1` docs, and null-output semantics. These are low-risk polish items and not required branch-change work for this scoped foundation PR. |

## Progress
- The previous `source_ref` validation action item was fixed on current head `6eb244410d18d8f5b8c08cd969ec4c7ad27849cb`.
- The current rubrics review passes and confirms the fix includes stricter validation, tests, and docs.
- Codex found no current BLOCKING/WARNING/INFO findings; Claude flagged two warnings plus INFO polish.
- Synthesis carries forward only the path fallback as required work; the SHA casing concern is treated as optional consistency cleanup.
- A new inline comment was posted for the path fallback issue; the existing inline `source_ref` comment is already addressed by the current head.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Document and test metadata path fallback</summary>

**Cause:**  
[`resolveSepoVersionMetadataPath`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/6eb244410d18d8f5b8c08cd969ec4c7ad27849cb/.agent/src/sepo-version.ts#L240) falls back from `.agent/sepo-version.json` to `sepo-version.json` in the current working directory without a test or documentation explaining that behavior.

**Candidate solutions:**  
Keep the fallback, add a test showing it supports commands run from `.agent/`, and document that lookup behavior in CLI/versioning docs. Alternatively, remove the fallback so missing `.agent/sepo-version.json` fails on the expected path.

**Comments:**  
The fallback may be intentional, but because this CLI becomes part of the installed metadata contract, silent alternate-path loading should be explicit and covered.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the remaining issue is a small, concrete branch change that an automated fix-pr pass can safely handle by documenting and testing the fallback behavior.

## Final Verdict
MINOR_ISSUES

## Action Items
- [ ] Document and test the `sepo-version.json` current-working-directory fallback, or remove the fallback so missing metadata fails clearly.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 6/10).

review verdict is minor_issues; dispatching fix-pr

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777864147879 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUyOTg4OTg0ODk6cmV2aWV3OjEyODpmaXhfcHI6MTI4OjY -->

**Sepo did not produce code changes for this PR.**

Please add more context or restate the requested fixes, then try again.

Use multiple small PRs, not one large PR.

Current best split:

1. **Foundation PR**: version policy docs, `.agent/sepo-version.json`, typed validation/loading helpers, CLI, focused tests. This is already represented by #127 / PR #128.
2. **Install/update PR**: update install and updater behavior to preserve/rewrite version metadata and use release tags as the default update target.
3. **Bug-report PR**: include Sepo version, source repo/ref/SHA, and agent file hash in failure reports.
4. **Release automation PR**: add prepare/publish workflows, with prepare opening a human-reviewed release PR and publish creating tags/releases.
5. **Branch/tag policy docs PR**, if not fully covered by the above: document `main`, optional `develop`, release tags, and RC policy.

I would not make this a deep stack unless needed for speed. Land the foundation first, then have later PRs target `main` after it merges. If parallel work is useful, later PRs can temporarily stack on #128, but they should be small and easy to retarget.

Sepo orchestration stopped after `fix-pr` concluded `no_changes`.

- Source action: `fix-pr`
- Source conclusion: `no_changes`
- Target: `pull_request #128`
- Round: `6/10`
- Reason: fix-pr concluded no_changes; no automatic handoff was dispatched because fix-pr must succeed before re-review
- Source run ID: `25299075157`

No follow-up workflow was dispatched. Inspect the source action status comment and workflow logs before retrying or continuing manually.

<!-- sepo-agent-orchestrate-stop -->

title: Add Sepo version metadata foundation
